title: Pali Term Record
description: Schema for a Pali term entry in the shiny-adventure translation dataset. Supports both lighter reference entries and major rule-bearing entries that encode translation policy, formula handling, provenance, and drift-prevention guidance.

notes.description: Optional explanation of the context-sensitive rule.

source.description: Optional citation such as SN 12.2 or DN 22.
Named authority, source profile, or internal OSF material that supports the policy.

scope.description: What part of the entry or translation policy this source supports.

priority.description: The source's authority layer in the house hierarchy.

kind.description: What sort of support the source provides.

notes.description: Short note on how the source is being used.

default_scope.description: Where the preferred_translation should normally govern.

when_not_to_apply.description: Where the preferred_translation should not be used without an explicit local override.

compound_inheritance.description: Whether related compounds inherit the headword policy by default.

leave_untranslated_when.description: Conditions under which leaving the term in Pali is preferred.

drift_risk.description: The main translation drift or doctrinal confusion this policy is meant to prevent.

term.description: The headword in Pāli, with diacritics where applicable.

normalized_term.description: ASCII or simplified lowercase form for filenames, search, and indexing.

entry_type.description: Classifies the entry as a major rule-bearing term or a minor term.

literal_meaning.description: Literal or etymologically transparent sense, when useful.

preferred_translation.description: Default preferred English rendering for this project. This is the live schema field for the repository's preferred rendering and should function as the governing default unless a context rule explicitly overrides it.

discouraged_translations.description: Renderings that should generally be avoided.

untranslated_preferred.description: Whether the Pāli term itself is generally preferred over translation.

gloss_on_first_occurrence.description: Suggested gloss for first occurrence in a text.

definition.description: Short explanatory definition for dictionary and glossary use. For major entries, this supports the rule record but does not replace it.

notes.description: Freeform usage notes and editorial rationale. This is part of the live rule-bearing structure for major entries and should explain what drift or confusion the rule is meant to prevent.

context_rules.description: Rules for when translation changes by context. This is the live schema field for translation rules and is strongly recommended for major entries. These rules should govern when the default applies, when it does not, and when compounds or formulas inherit the headword.

related_terms.description: Other Pāli headwords related semantically, doctrinally, or grammatically. These links help maintain rule propagation across doctrinal families, compounds, and formula clusters.

example_phrases.description: Example phrases or passages containing the term. This is the live schema field for examples and is strongly recommended for major entries. Examples should demonstrate the governed translation decision rather than merely repeat the headword.

sutta_references.description: Canonical references where the term is especially important.

tags.description: Project tags for grouping terms by theme or function.

authority_basis.description: Structured provenance for the entry's translation policy and rationale.

translation_policy.description: Structured summary of how the term's default rendering governs, when it should yield, and what drift the rule is designed to prevent.

version_notes.description: Optional note about changes to the entry over time.
